impact_on_program.next: Prove class (iii) empty in true residual, or pay (i)+(ii) and bound (iii); or prove residual Phi_w injectivity on C_res.
impact_on_program.payment_split: tight seam | partial plant | coset-free non-tight

criterion.name: atom_criterion_Kres_only
criterion.note: Do not substitute global M_m lower bounds for K_res (v10). Primary target K_res=1 restores U_res <= target/17.
criterion.statement: If max_z M_m^{res,side}(z) <= K_res and max_z U_res(z) <= floor(target/(pack * K_res)), then max_z |R(z)| <= target (residual lex covering, v4).

definitions.name: residual_can_core_definitions
proof: All maps are well-defined from the v3 lex-split and monic locator maps.
C_res is the residual can-core family; N_can_prim is its cardinality.
definitions.statement:
Fix residual family R(z) subset Fib_w^{(j)}(z). For S in R(z) let U(S) be the e=w+1 lex-smallest elements of S, C_can(S)=S\\U(S), u(S)=Phi_w(Lambda_{U(S)}), b(S)=Phi_w(Lambda_{C_can(S)}). Define:
  C_res(z) = { C_can(S) : S in R(z) }
  U_res(z) = |{ u(S) : S in R(z) }|
  N_can_prim(z) = |C_res(z)|
  M_m^{res,side}(z) = max_u |{ C_can(S) : S in R(z), u(S)=u }|
  M_m^{res,phi}(z) = max_b |{ C in C_res(z) : Phi_w(C)=b }|
  M_m^{res,max} = max_z M_m^{res,side}(z)  (routing K_res; also M_m^{res,phi} <= M_m^{res,side} is false in general, but both are <= global M_m).

multimate_dichotomy.name: residual_multimate_dichotomy
proof: v9 intersection law and tight-pair characterization apply to any m-subsets in a common Phi_w fiber; restrict to C_res.
v9 tight-clique packing: free-1 CS (w+1)-blocks are pairwise disjoint, so at most 1+floor((n−m)/(w+1)) pad onto one core R0.
multimate_dichotomy.statement:
Let C ≠ C' be in C_res(z) with Phi_w(C)=Phi_w(C'). Then |C ∩ C'| <= m − w − 1. Moreover:
  TIGHT: |C ∩ C'| = m − w − 1  ⇔  R0=C∩C' has size m−(w+1) and U=C\\C', V=C'\\C form a free-1 constant-shift pair of (w+1)-sets;
  NON-TIGHT: |C ∩ C'| < m − w − 1.
Any pairwise-tight subfamily of C_res(z) has size <= k_tight = 1+floor((n−m)/(w+1)) = 18.
multimate_dichotomy.warning: k_tight bounds only pairwise-tight residual subfamilies, not full M_m^{res} (non-tight multi-mates can be larger — v9 toys).

partial_plant_class.name: partial_plant_multimate_class
partial_plant_class.open: No proved first-match payment yet deletes all partial-plant can-cores from residual. Coset-free multi-mates remain even after such a payment.
proof: v8: each pad mate is R cup U with U a full c-coset => max_Q >= 1.
v10: maximal terminal planted j-supports excluded from R(z); single-fiber pads are not maximal.
If every residual can-core is fiber-free for all c >= w+1, then partial-plant type multi-mates cannot occur in C_res.
partial_plant_class.statement: Call a residual multi-mate pair (C,C') partial-plant type if at least one of C,C' has max_Q >= 1 for some c|n with c >= w+1. The v8 coset-pad global multi-mates are partial-plant type with c=131072, |Q|=1. Maximal terminal plants are already non-residual (v10). A first-match cell that pays all j-supports whose can-core is partial-plant type would force every residual multi-mate pair to be coset-free (no full c-fiber factor on either core).

payment_law.name: residual_multimate_payment_law
payment_law.program: Pay (i) and (ii) by first-match cells; prove (iii) cannot occur in true residual (or is size-1) to get M_m^{res}<=1.
proof: Dichotomy (i) vs not-(i) is the tight/non-tight split (lemma multimate).
Among non-tight (and also among tight) pairs, either some core has a c-fiber factor (ii) or both are fiber-free for all c>=w+1 (iii).
These three classes partition residual multi-mate pairs.

program.name: v11_program_reduction
proof: Global uniqueness dead; residual uniqueness open.
Multi-mate payment law partitions the obstruction.
program.statement: After v8–v10, the residual atom reduces to: prove M_m^{res,side} <= 1 (or small K_res), OR bound U_phi (residual can-core Phi_w-image) directly, OR pay all residual multi-mate classes (i)+(ii)+(iii) in first-match.

routing.name: residual_routing_side_and_phi
routing.note: v7 B2 bounds U_phi; residual uniqueness bounds M_m^{res,*}. Either route closes the atom with lex covering |R| <= pack * N_can_prim.
proof: Side: partition C_res by u(S); each block size <= M_m^{res,side}; number of blocks <= U_res. (v5/v10 residual routing, residual-restricted.)
Phi: partition C_res by Phi_w(C); each block size <= M_m^{res,phi}; number of blocks = U_phi.
Global comparison: residual cores are a subfamily of all m-subsets.
routing.statement: N_can_prim(z) <= U_res(z) * M_m^{res,side}(z). Also N_can_prim(z) <= U_phi(z) * M_m^{res,phi}(z) where U_phi(z) = |{ Phi_w(C) : C in C_res(z) }| (residual can-core Phi_w-image size; the v7 B2 object). Always M_m^{res,side}(z) <= M_m^{max} and M_m^{res,phi}(z) <= M_m^{max}.

title: Residual can-core family, multi-mate classes, K_res criterion
